confirmed: The Atmosphere candidate-lane README existed at the pinned base and was updated in place.

The domain-atmosphere workflow checks that this lane contains no non-README candidate record and retains explicit validation, proof, and release-dry-run holds.
The Atmosphere release index is a draft navigation surface whose release register is intentionally empty and whose examples are illustrative rather than real releases.
The Atmosphere proof parent and pm25_2026 child are documented, but concrete proof inventory, schemas, validators, fixtures, access controls, and release linkage remain unverified.
The Atmosphere test lane is a scaffold, the domain policy README is a greenfield scaffold, and the general release-dry-run workflow remains TODO-only.
The published Atmosphere README defines a release-gated carrier boundary while emitted artifact, manifest, and governed-route inventory remains unverified.
CODEOWNERS routes release review to bartytime4life while explicitly denying that routing is stewardship, review completion, release approval, or publication authority.
proposed: release/candidates/atmosphere/ should operate as a repository-grounded, freshness-aware Atmosphere candidate index and pre-publication review contract without becoming source, evidence, policy, review, release, regulatory, medical, emergency, or publication authority.

authority_boundary: This receipt records an AI-authored documentation revision. It is not an Atmosphere candidate, SourceDescriptor, EvidenceBundle, PolicyDecision, ReviewRecord, ValidationReport, PromotionDecision, ReleaseManifest, CorrectionNotice, WithdrawalNotice, RollbackCard, AQI product, medical advice, regulatory determination, emergency alert, steward approval, release approval, publication authority, or proof of runtime behavior.

rollback.strategy: Close the draft pull request before merge, or revert the generated-receipt commit and README commit in reverse order after merge.
